name: VoxCPM2 Complete
description: Full offline VoxCPM2 runtime package for Storyboard Copilot. Includes a portable Python runtime bridge plus a local VoxCPM2 model directory aligned with the latest upstream VoxCPM2 workflow.

hardwareRequirements.summary: VoxCPM2 now targets multilingual 48 kHz speech generation, voice design, controllable voice cloning, and ultimate cloning. The upstream README recommends Python 3.10-3.12, PyTorch 2.5+, CUDA 12+, and roughly 8 GB of VRAM for comfortable inference.
recommendations: Recommended: an NVIDIA GPU with CUDA 12 support for faster inference and warmup.
The packaged runtime is large because it includes a portable Python environment plus the full VoxCPM2 model snapshot.
Keep enough free disk space for runtime cache, generated WAV files, and repeated warmup retries.
notes: This package is validated against the current upstream voxcpm 2.0.2 Python package.
Reference clips work best when they are single-speaker, clean, and around 5-15 seconds.
The template is assembled into build/extensions/voxcpm2-complete and is not intended to be loaded directly before packaging.

startupSteps.label: Validate package
startupSteps.description: Check the runtime entry and model declarations.

startupSteps.label: Verify runtime
startupSteps.description: Check Python runtime and VoxCPM package availability.

startupSteps.label: Warm up runtime
startupSteps.description: Load the local VoxCPM2 model into memory before exposing the nodes.

startupSteps.label: Register nodes
startupSteps.description: Expose VoxCPM nodes to the canvas.
